docs.title: Scanners
docs.text: CHERENKOV's scanning engine uses a plugin-based architecture. All scanners inherit from BaseScanner and are auto-discovered via the ScannerRegistry.

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• Plugin Architecture — Drop-in scanner modules with standard interfaces
  • Auto-Discovery — Scanners are automatically registered at startup
  • Chainable — Scanners can compose and pipeline results
  • Validation Gate — Every result passes through TOKAMAK proof validation

docs.title: Scanner Strategy
docs.text: CHERENKOV uses an intelligent scan strategy system that adapts to each target.

docs.title: Strategy Types
docs.text: Strategy Description Use Case Rapid Fast, high-signal checks CI/CD pipelines Deep Comprehensive coverage Audit / compliance Stealth Low-and-slow, evasion-aware Red team exercises Adaptive AI-guided, learns from response Unknown targets

docs.title: How Strategy Works
docs.text:
1. TENSOR analyzes target profile (technology, exposure, compliance reqs)
  2. Agent Governor selects optimal strategy
  3. KINETIC executes scanners based on strategy priorities
  4. Results feed back into LATTICE for future strategy optimization

docs.title: Third-Party Wrappers
docs.text: CHERENKOV supports wrapping third-party scanning tools as CHERENKOV scanners. This allows you to integrate existing tools into the Trident workflow.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Create a wrapper class that inherits from BaseScanner
  2. The wrapper runs the third-party tool via subprocess or API
  3. Output is parsed and converted to ScanResult format
  4. Results pass through the Validation Gate like native scanners

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
class NucleiWrapper(BaseScanner):
    name = "nuclei"
    description = "Nuclei template-based scanner"

    async def scan(self, target: str) -> ScanResult:
        result = await run_subprocess(["nuclei", "-u", target, "-json"])
        return parse_nuclei_output(result)

docs.title: Supported Wrappers
docs.text:
• Nuclei
  • Zapier / ZAP
  • Custom tools via CLI or REST API

docs.title: Validated Scanners
docs.text: CHERENKOV ships with a growing library of validated scanners. These scanners have been tested against known targets and produce TOKAMAK-verifiable results.

docs.title: Current Inventory
docs.text: Scanner File Category CWE Status HTTP Security Headers header_scanner.py Web CWE-693, CWE-16 ✅ Validated Security Header Analysis security_headers.py Web CWE-693 ✅ Validated Dangerous HTTP Methods http_methods.py Web CWE-749 ✅ Validated TLS Version Detection tls_detection.py Web/TLS CWE-326, CWE-327 ✅ Validated Unified Web Scanner unified_scanner.py Web Multiple ✅ Validated

docs.title: Target: 20 Validated Scanners (Phase 3)
docs.text: The Phase 3 roadmap targets 20 validated scanners covering OWASP Top 10, API, and infrastructure categories. Mobile (Android/iOS) scanners are planned for Phase 4.

docs.title: Validation Gate
docs.text: The Validation Gate is the quality checkpoint between scanner output and final report. Every HIGH and CRITICAL finding must pass through TOKAMAK before it reaches the report.

docs.title: Gate Process
docs.text:
1. Scanner submits finding → Gate intercepts
  2. Finding severity checked → HIGH/CRITICAL flagged for validation
  3. TOKAMAK executes PoC in sandbox
  4. Pass → Finding included in report, cryptographically signed
  5. Fail → Finding downgraded or removed

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Zero false positives on HIGH/CRITICAL findings
  • Cryptographic proof for every reported finding
  • Audit trail — every gate decision is logged and signed

docs.title: Writing a Scanner
docs.text: Scanners are Python classes that inherit from BaseScanner. They are auto-discovered and registered by the ScannerRegistry.

docs.title: Basic Template
docs.text:
from cherenkov.core.base_scanner import BaseScanner
from cherenkov.core.models import ScanResult

class MyScanner(BaseScanner):
    name = "my-scanner"
    description = "Detects XYZ vulnerabilities"

    async def scan(self, target: str) -> ScanResult:
        # Your scanning logic here
        return ScanResult(
            scanner=self.name,
            target=target,
            findings=[...]
        )

docs.title: Registration
docs.text: Place your scanner file in the scanners/ directory. It will be auto-discovered on next startup.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Must implement async def scan(self, target: str) -> ScanResult
  • Must set name and description class attributes
  • Must return a ScanResult with typed findings
  • Should handle errors gracefully and return partial results on failure

docs.title: Security
docs.text: CHERENKOV takes security seriously. This section covers our security model and vulnerability disclosure process.

docs.title: Vulnerability Disclosure
docs.text: If you discover a security vulnerability in CHERENKOV, please follow our disclosure process.

docs.title: Reporting
docs.text:
1. Do not open a public GitHub issue
  2. Email details to info@cherenkov-security.com
  3. Include steps to reproduce and impact assessment

docs.title: Process
docs.text: Step Expected Timeline Acknowledgment 48 hours Initial assessment 5 business days Fix development Based on severity Public disclosure After fix is released

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Core framework (src/cherenkov/)
  • Default scanner plugins
  • API and CLI
  • Docker deployment

Out of scope: third-party tools integrated via wrappers.
